body_txt: Owning and managing a restaurant is a demanding job. Its operation requires people to expend considerable money, time, and effort—and even that, in most cases, won't be enough to keep it from failing. When characters on a TV show open or buy a restaurant, they usually do so thinking it will be fun, without taking into account the amount of money, work, red tape, and stress involved. This results in the characters being quickly overwhelmed by their new responsibilities and comically blundering through a series of culinary and serving mishaps that make their inexperience in the food service industry apparent to the unfortunate customers. In most instances, the episode ends with the character closing or selling the restaurant and going back to whatever job they held previously. Sub-Trope of New Job Episode . Compare Lemonade Stand Plot , where one or more characters start their own lemonade stand.
